GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "Ring in summer with State of Light wines", "Meet the low-alcohol, low-calorie, full-flavoured wines to enjoy now.", "The arrival of an unforeseen global pandemic has meant we have all faced significant challenges this year, and dealt with them in the best ways we can. We've adjusted to new routines, made local travel plans in place of cancelled international getaways, celebrated weddings, birthdays and births over Zoom, and found new ways to rejig our exercise regimens at home. In the midst of these unprecedented changes, however, Covid-19 has also been a grounding experience for many, and has reconnected people all over the world with friends, old and new, hobbies, and ways of life more in tune with our overall health, positivity and wellbeing. In the wake of the coronavirus, people now more than ever are recognising what is most important to them and relishing those things that bring them joy, whatever they may be. Gone are the days of 'guilty pleasures' - now, we're taking time to (safely) indulge in and appreciate whatever activities make us happy - be it scrolling through TikTok, learning how to bake bread or enjoying a glass, or two, of wine to the fullest. And why should we have it any other way? Or so says State of Light, a new wine with a like minded approach, premised on giving consumers a healthier alternative and guilt-free license to indulge in their drink of choice. Created specifically with the modern woman's lifestyle in mind, State of Light is a premium alcohol brand, specialising in wine that prides itself on low-calorie, low-alcohol content without compromising on flavour. After recognising rising consumer demand for wine that meets women's needs and fits in with their ever-changing lifestyles, and that can be enjoyed socially as well as on one's own. State of Light has developed three wines that boast 35 percent less calories and 45 percent less alcohol than the average 13% alcohol wine, In line with changing consumer habits, we are seeing women and men become more mindful about their overall health and alcohol consumption, and they are striving to achieve better balance in their lives. While State of Light's Pinot Gris and Rose were developed in New Zealand's Hawke's Bay, the oldest wine region on the north island, the Sauvignon Blanc is from the Marlborough region in New Zealand's South Island, attesting to its rich and diverse history of winemaking. The Sauvignon Blanc features a flavour profile that includes notes of passionfruit and guava, capped off with a citrus finish, whereas the Pinot Gris lends itself to a more floral palette with notes of apple and pear. Finally, the Rose, perfect for sipping on during summer days that turn into nights, heroes berry fruits and watermelon aromatics for a fresh yet elegant finish. And should you be inclined to presume that State of Light's lower alcohol content may dilute its taste, the label's head winemaker, Brett Fullerton, has debunked this myth. As Fullerton explains: "a common perception is that all lower alcohol wines are poor quality and have less flavour, which is simply not true. The State of Light winemaking team take pride in producing lighter, full-flavoured premium wines that celebrate the renowned New Zealand Marlborough and Hawke's Bay regions." So, as you pour a glass to enjoy alone or with friends, remember that only the alcoholic and calorie content has been eliminated in parts, but none of the fun or the flavour, so you can enjoy State of Light, worry-free."
